title: The Structure of Scientific Revolutions
excerpt: The most influential account of how science actually progresses — not through steady accumulation but through revolutionary paradigm shifts that transform how we see the world.
keyIdeas: Normal science as puzzle-solving within a paradigm
keyTakeaways: Science does not progress linearly — it advances through revolutions that overturn established frameworks
Paradigms define what counts as a problem, a solution, and a fact
Paradigm choice is not purely rational — it involves persuasion, values, and community consensus
After a revolution, scientists work in a different world than before
whoShouldRead: Anyone curious about how science really works
whoShouldSkip: Those seeking a technical philosophy text
Readers who believe science is purely objective and cumulative
People looking for practical research methods
